headline: MSTR pinned by long-gamma structure near 171 as call skew persists across the curve; TSLA sandwiched between the 375 put wall and 380 call wall with IV at a 25th-percentile low, tilting the edge decisively to premium buyers.

setup.gamma_note: Net GEX +6.2B; market makers are net long gamma, mechanically selling into rallies and buying dips, creating a gravitational pin in the 165–185 corridor

bull.path: BTC recovers above $80K, lifting MSTR through the 180 call wall and into the 185–195 GEX call cluster; IV expands on the directional break as short-call hedging accelerates the move.
triggers: BTC closes above $80,000 on daily basis
MSTR daily close above 180 on volume greater than 1.5× 20-day average

base.path: Spot oscillates in the 165–185 range pinned by long-gamma structure; call skew gradually compresses as the week progresses; front IV drifts lower from 76% toward 70% with time decay; BTC stabilizes near current levels.
triggers: MSTR holds above 165 GEX put floor without a confirmed close below

bear.path: BTC weakness pushes MSTR below the 165 GEX put wall, triggering negative dealer delta-hedging flows that amplify the selloff; tests the 155–160 zone where the next major put support sits.
triggers: MSTR closes below 165 on above-average volume

trades.rationale: Fade persistent call skew (70th percentile) directly at the 185 GEX call wall (second-largest call gamma cluster at +1.07B); NVRP 1.11 confirms seller edge with IV outpacing realized vol; long-gamma regime suppresses sustained breakouts through this level; the anomalous -11.2 pt call skew in the May 29 bucket signals speculative call accumulation that is structurally prone to unwind.
trades.exit: Take profit at 50% of max gain; stop if MSTR closes above 187 on two consecutive sessions, or if NVRP falls below 0.95

trades.rationale: The 165 GEX put wall carries -677M in dealer short gamma, providing mechanical support via long-gamma hedging flows; selling the 160/150 spread collects elevated put premium while staying 3.5% below the GEX floor; BTC beta 1.4 context consistent with base-case sideways action.
trades.exit: Take profit at 50% of max gain; stop if MSTR closes below 163 or if BTC breaks $74,000 on a daily close

risks: BTC correlation at beta 1.4 means a sharp BTC selloff directly cascades to MSTR regardless of the options structure
May 29 bucket anomalous call skew of -11.2 pts could reflect informed buying ahead of an undisclosed catalyst
Long-gamma pinning breaks down rapidly once spot exits the 165–185 range; both spreads need active monitoring at the GEX boundaries
exits: Close all short premium positions immediately if NVRP drops below 0.95 (buyer edge returned, seller edge lost)
Roll short strikes outward by 10 points if spot threatens a GEX-wall breach confirmed by two consecutive closes outside the 165–185 range

setup.gamma_note: Net GEX +12.4B but classified neutral; spot sits in a thin no-man's-land between the 375 put wall (0.3% OTM) and 380 call wall (0.98% OTM), creating coiled tension where any directional catalyst will be amplified by dealer hedging

bull.path: TSLA pushes through the 380 GEX call wall; negative-gamma dealer short-covering amplifies the break; rally targets 390–400 where the two largest call GEX walls at +4.79B and +8.07B cap the move.
triggers: TSLA daily close above 380 with options volume spike above 130% of 20-day average
Broader equity rally with SPX gaining 1%+ on a single session

base.path: Spot remains trapped in the 370–385 corridor bounded by major GEX walls on both sides; realized vol compresses toward front IV of 39%; low IV environment persists with no macro catalyst.
triggers: No significant macro event this week
TSLA holds 370 and fails to clear 385 for three or more consecutive sessions

bear.path: Spot breaks below the 370 GEX put wall; negative dealer delta flows amplify the move lower; tests the 350–360 GEX put cluster where the next major support at -3.65B sits.
triggers: TSLA closes below 370 on volume above 130% of 20-day average
Macro risk-off event pushing broader tech and high-beta names lower

trades.rationale: NVRP 0.87 signals IV (38.96%) is priced below realized vol (RV30 44.70%), a rare buyer-edge condition; spot is literally pinned at the 375/380 GEX intersection — the tightest wall configuration in the data — making a directional break imminent; IV at the 25th percentile 2-year low makes this strangle historically cheap; 370 put and 385 call bracket the two most likely break points where dealer hedging accelerates the move.
trades.exit: Close each leg independently at 2× its individual cost; cut the losing leg at 50% loss if spot moves strongly through one GEX wall and holds for two sessions

trades.rationale: 86.6% of open call interest is concentrated in short-dated contracts (speculative regime), signaling demand for upside exposure; a break above the 380 GEX call wall (only 0.98% OTM) targets the 400 level where +8.07B call GEX creates the dominant ceiling; NVRP 0.87 makes this debit spread cheap relative to realized vol; 25 DTE captures delayed follow-through from a bullish break.
trades.exit: Close at 75% of max profit ($15 spread width minus debit) or when TSLA touches 400; stop at full debit loss if TSLA closes below 373 on two consecutive days

risks: Long strangle is exposed to continued low-IV mean reversion if spot stays pinned — theta decay erodes value even when the buyer edge setup is structurally correct
The 375 put wall (0.3% OTM) and 380 call wall (0.98% OTM) are so close that spot could oscillate between them for multiple sessions, bleeding both strangle legs
Broader macro overhang (tariffs, Fed policy, EV demand headlines) can amplify a downside break well beyond the GEX framework's implied range
exits: Strangle: manage each leg independently — take profit at 2× individual leg cost; exit the losing leg if TSLA decisively breaks one GEX wall and consolidates for two sessions
Call spread: close for 75% of max gain; stop at full debit loss if TSLA fails to reclaim 376 within five sessions of entering
